front: What is the definition of a regular stochastic matrix?
back: A stochastic matrix P is called regular if some power Pᵏ (for some k ≥ 1) has all entries strictly positive (i.e., every entry > 0).

front: What is the fundamental property of a regular stochastic matrix regarding its steady-state vector?
back: For any regular stochastic matrix P, there exists a unique steady-state vector q such that Pq = q. Furthermore, for any initial probability vector v, the sequence v, vP, vP², vP³,... converges to q as n → ∞.

front: How can you identify if a stochastic matrix is regular by examining its associated Markov chain?
back: A stochastic matrix is regular if its associated Markov chain is irreducible (every state can be reached from every other state) and aperiodic (the greatest common divisor of the lengths of paths from a state back to itself is 1).

front: What is the practical implication of a Markov chain having a regular transition matrix?
back: It means the system has a long-term stable behavior independent of the starting state. The chain will converge to a unique stationary distribution, which describes the proportion of time the process spends in each state over the long run.

front: True or False: Every stochastic matrix with all positive entries is regular.
back: True. If every entry pᵢⱼ > 0, then the matrix itself satisfies the condition for regularity (where k=1). It is a sufficient, but not necessary, condition for regularity.
